unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns the words for games and play — action words like bounce, roll, throw and catch. They learn the names of parts of the body, and words that say where things are (on, under, next to), and left and right. They play simple games, learn a game from another country, and listen to a funny story. Along the way they hear the short 'u' sound (sun, fun, duck). The goal is happy, active English — learning through play.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

visual.alt: Child-friendly illustration for Fun and Games

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To make something jump up again after it hits the ground.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I bounce the ball outside.
practiceSentences: The ball can bounce very high.
Can you bounce the ball twice?
Yes, watch me bounce it!
I bounce my ball in the garden.
Balls bounce well on hard ground.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: b - o - u - n - c - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say bounce slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To move along by turning over and over.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I roll the ball to Musa.
practiceSentences: Roll the ball to your friend.
Can you roll the ball to me?
Yes, I will roll it now!
I roll my ball down the hill.

dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: r - o - l - l
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say roll slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To send something through the air with your hand.

Can you throw it to me?
Yes, I will throw it now!

dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: t - h - r - o - w
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say throw slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To take hold of something moving in the air.

practiceSentences: Musa can catch the big ball.

dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: c - a - t - c - h
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say catch slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To knock something with your hand or with a bat.

dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: h - i - t
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say hit slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To move your legs very fast, faster than walking.

practiceSentences: The children run across the field.

dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say run slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: j - u - m - p
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say jump slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To hit your hands together to make a sound.

dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say clap slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To move something quickly from side to side.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The part at the end of your arm with five fingers.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Name and act out ball actions (bounce, roll, throw, catch, hit).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Name parts of the body (hand, foot, ear, nose, shoulder, head…).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say where something is (on, under, next to) and show left and right.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Ask and answer “Can you ___?” with “Yes, I can.” / “No, I can't.”
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Follow simple game instructions and take turns fairly.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Hear and say words with the short ‘u’ sound (sun, fun, duck, bug).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

readings.title: Amal and the Big Ball
readings.theme: movement, body parts, position and fair play
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
It was a sunny day in the town of Hargeisa. Amal ran outside to play. She had a big red ball.

“Look at my ball!” said Amal. She could bounce it. Bounce, bounce, bounce!

Her friend Samira came to play. “Can you catch the ball?” asked Amal.

“Yes, I can!” said Samira. Amal did throw the ball. Samira did catch it. “Well done!” said Amal.

Then little Ishmael came. He was only four. “Can I play too?” asked Ishmael.

Amal looked at the ball. She wanted to play with Samira. But Ishmael looked sad.

“Yes!” said Amal. “Let us all play. We can take turns.”

Amal did roll the ball to Ishmael. Roll, roll, roll! Ishmael put his two hands on the ball. He was happy.

“Now throw it to me!” said Samira. Ishmael did throw the ball. But — oh no! The ball went up, up, up!

The ball went into a big tree. It was on a branch. “My ball is stuck!” said Amal.

The three friends looked up. How could they get the ball?

“I know!” said Samira. “Let us jump!” They all did jump. Jump, jump, jump! But the ball was too high.

Just then, big brother Adam came. He was tall. “I can help,” said Adam.

Adam did shake the branch. Shake, shake, shake! The ball came down. Amal did catch it. “Thank you, Adam!” said all the children.

“You shared the ball,” said Adam. “You are good friends.”

Amal, Samira and Ishmael played all day. They did bounce and roll and catch. It was so much fun!

When the sun went down, Amal said, “Sharing is the best game of all.”

readings.title: Talk about Fun and Games
readings.theme: movement, body parts, position and fair play
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. I can ___. Can you ___? This is my ___. The ball is on the ___. Touch your ___. I am ___ing. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: Fun and Games rhyme
readings.theme: movement, body parts, position and fair play
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
The “Bounce the Ball” poem — say it together
Bounce the ball, / Ball, ball, / Bounce the ball! / Roll it, throw it, / Catch the ball!
Clap the rhythm as you say it. If you have a ball, bounce it as you read. Say it three times — a little faster each time!
The “Wind the Bobbin Up” action song
Wind the bobbin up. / Wind the bobbin up. / Pull, pull. Clap, clap, clap. / Point to the ceiling. / Point to the floor. / Point to the window and point to the door. / Clap your hands together, 1, 2, 3. / Put your hands upon your knees.
Do every action as you sing. Point to the ceiling, the floor, the window and the door. This is a wonderful way to learn body and action words with your whole body.
Little rhyme: “I can run like a puppy” (short ‘u’ sound)
I can run like a puppy. / I can jump like a bug. / I can buzz like a bee. / I can sit on the rug.

comprehension.question: What colour was Amal's big ball?
comprehension.explanation: The story says Amal had a big red ball.

comprehension.question: In which town was Amal playing?
comprehension.explanation: The first line says it was a sunny day in the town of Hargeisa.

comprehension.question: What happened to the ball after Ishmael threw it?
comprehension.correctAnswer: It went up into a big tree
comprehension.explanation: Ishmael threw the ball, it went up, up, up, and landed on a branch.

comprehension.question: Amal said, 'My ball is stuck!' Where was it stuck?
comprehension.correctAnswer: On a branch of the big tree
comprehension.explanation: The ball went into a big tree and stayed on a branch.

comprehension.question: How did Ishmael feel when he asked to play? How do you know?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Sad, because the story says Ishmael looked sad
comprehension.explanation: Ishmael asks 'Can I play too?' and the story says he looked sad.

comprehension.question: Why could Adam get the ball down when the children could not?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Because he was tall and could shake the branch
comprehension.explanation: The story says Adam was tall, and he shook the branch until the ball fell.

comprehension.question: Which talk line tells where the ball is?
comprehension.explanation: 'The ball is on the ___' is the position line in the talk lines.

comprehension.question: Say 'Touch your ___' and touch your nose.
comprehension.explanation: 'Touch your ___' is the body-part line you say while acting.

comprehension.question: Which talk line asks a friend if they can do something?
comprehension.explanation: 'Can you ___?' is the question line for asking a friend.

comprehension.question: Jump, and say the talk line 'I am ___ing.'
comprehension.explanation: 'I am ___ing' is the line for saying what you are doing now.

comprehension.question: Finish this talk line: 'I can ___.'
comprehension.explanation: The first talk line is 'I can ___,' for an action you can do.

comprehension.question: Hold up your ball and say, 'This is my ___.'
comprehension.explanation: The talk line 'This is my ___' is for showing something of your own.

grammar.title: Tell what you can do at play
grammar.explanation: Choose a game action you are good at. Say the sentence, then show it with your body.
grammar.commonMistake: Some children answer with only the action, like 'hop'. Ask kindly for the whole sentence: 'I can hop.'
grammar.memoryTip: Do the action while you say it. Your body helps your mouth remember.
grammar.practice: Say and do three: I can hop. I can catch. I can climb.

grammar.title: Ask a friend 'Can you...?'
grammar.explanation: Swap the words around to make a question. Wait for your friend to answer 'Yes, I can.'
grammar.commonMistake: Children often say 'You can hop?' To ask, 'Can' jumps to the front: 'Can you hop?'
grammar.memoryTip: Asking flips it over: 'I can' becomes 'Can you?'
grammar.practice: Ask a friend three questions: Can you skip? Can you kick? Can you jump high?

grammar.title: Show your toy and name it
grammar.explanation: Hold up your own toy and tell the class about it. 'My' means the toy belongs to you.
grammar.commonMistake: Sometimes 'my' is left out: 'This is ball.' Add 'my' when the toy is your own.
grammar.memoryTip: Hug the toy close as you say 'my', because close things are yours.
grammar.practice: Show three of your things: This is my ball. This is my kite. This is my skipping rope.

grammar.title: Say where the ball is
grammar.explanation: Put a ball somewhere, then tell us where it sits. 'On' means it is resting on top of something.
grammar.commonMistake: 'On' and 'in' get swapped often. On means on top; in means inside. Move the ball and check together.
grammar.memoryTip: Place your hand flat on your head. That is 'on top'.
grammar.practice: Move a ball and say three: The ball is on the mat. The ball is on the chair. The ball is on the table.

grammar.title: Give a body-part instruction
grammar.explanation: Give your friend a fun order and watch them do it quickly. Then swap over and take a turn.
grammar.commonMistake: Children may say 'Touch nose.' The word 'your' tells us whose nose it is.
grammar.memoryTip: Play it like a game, saying it faster and faster each time.
grammar.practice: Give three instructions: Touch your nose. Touch your knees. Touch your ears.

grammar.title: Say what you are doing right now
grammar.explanation: Talk about what is happening this very moment. Add -ing to the end of the action word.
grammar.commonMistake: The word 'am' often disappears: 'I running.' Both parts are needed: 'I am running.'
grammar.memoryTip: -ing means happening now, so keep moving while you say it.
grammar.practice: Do an action and say three: I am running. I am jumping. I am clapping.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Copy an adult's action, then say what you did. Say: 'I can run.' 'I can jump.' 'I can hop on one foot.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I can ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Ask a friend to try an action, then answer them. Say: 'Can you skip?' 'Yes, I can skip.' 'No, I cannot skip yet.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Can you ___? Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Show your partner a body part or a toy and tell them about it. Say: 'This is my hand.' 'This is my foot.' 'This is my ball.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern This is my ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Picture talk
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Move a ball around the room and say where it is. Say: 'The ball is on the chair.' 'The ball is under the table.' 'The ball is in the box.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern The ball is on the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Play a giving-orders game with a friend. Say: 'Touch your head.' 'Touch your knees.' 'Touch your nose, then sit down.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Touch your ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Tell the class what you are doing while you do it. Say: 'I am jumping.' 'I am clapping.' 'I am playing with Yusuf.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I am ___ing. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw your favourite game with a friend in it. Tell an adult what you both do and how you take turns fairly.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace the action words 'run', 'jump' and 'clap'. Do the action once after each word, then trace all three again.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy
writing.promptAndInstructions: Match hand, foot and head to the right places on a body picture. Then copy the word 'hand' twice.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw a box with a ball beside it. Add two labels: 'ball' and 'box'. Then tell an adult where the ball is.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.promptAndInstructions: Copy and finish twice: 'Touch your ___.' Use head and toes. Read your two orders to a friend so they can follow them.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose an action picture. Write one sentence ending in -ing, such as 'I am running.' Add a full stop and read it aloud.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

activities.title: Activity 1
activities.instructionsAndItems: Point to four toys in the picture: ball, kite, blocks, skipping rope. Say 'I like to play with the ___' for each one.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 2
activities.instructionsAndItems: Colour the ball, then play with a real or pretend one. Bounce it, roll it and catch it, saying the action word each time.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 3
activities.instructionsAndItems: Draw a line from each word to the right body part: head, arm, leg, foot. Then touch that part on yourself and say it.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 4
activities.instructionsAndItems: Play a moving game. An adult calls: 1. jump, 2. hop, 3. run on the spot, 4. freeze. Do each move and name it.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 5
activities.instructionsAndItems: Look at the animal pictures. Point to each animal and say its name and its move: 'The cat runs.' 'The bird flies.' 'The fish swims.'
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 6
activities.instructionsAndItems: Hide a ball for a friend to find. Put it on a box, then under a chair, then next to a bag. Ask: 'Where is the ball?'
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

assignments.title: My Fun and Games show-and-tell page
assignments.instructions: Share a game you love, the way Musa and Samira play. 1) Draw yourself playing, or bring a ball or soft toy. 2) Trace two words, such as jump, catch, hand or foot. 3) Put your ball somewhere and film a short clip saying: This is my ___. The ball is on the ___. I can bounce. I am running. 4) Touch and name three body parts. 5) Grown-ups, write which game made your child laugh most.
assignments.submissionType: Drawing or object + labels + short audio/video + reflection

quizzes.question: Which word is an action word, something you do?
quizzes.explanation: Jump is something your body does; a table, rain and a shoe are naming words, not actions.

quizzes.question: Which action do you do by putting your two hands together?
quizzes.explanation: Clapping uses two hands; you run and jump with your legs and blink with your eyes.

quizzes.question: A ball hits the ground and comes back up. Which word tells what it does?
quizzes.explanation: Going down and up again is called bouncing; sleeping, eating and writing are not ball movements.

quizzes.question: Musa throws a ball to Aisha. What should Aisha do with her hands?
quizzes.options: hide it | catch it | drop it | throw it
quizzes.explanation: You catch a ball that comes to you; throwing, dropping and hiding it would end the game.

quizzes.question: Which word means to move very fast on your legs?
quizzes.explanation: Running is fast leg movement; sitting is still, and looking and listening use your eyes and ears.

quizzes.question: Which one is a question?
quizzes.options: The ball is red. | We catch the ball. | I can catch the ball. | Can you catch the ball?
quizzes.explanation: Only this one asks something and ends with a question mark; the others are telling sentences.

quizzes.question: The ball is under the table. Which word tells you where the ball is?
quizzes.explanation: Under is the position word; ball and table are naming words and red is a colour.

quizzes.question: Which word completes the instruction: Touch your ___.
quizzes.explanation: You can touch a body part like your nose; jump is an action, fast tells speed and blue is a colour.

quizzes.question: What is a kind thing to say when you play a game with your friends?
quizzes.options: Go away. | You cannot play. | It is your turn. | I always win.
quizzes.explanation: Sharing turns is kind and fair; the other sentences would leave a friend out.

quizzes.question: Which word rhymes with hop?
quizzes.explanation: Hop and top both end with the /op/ sound; hat, hen and sun end in different sounds.

liveSessions.title: Meet the words
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring crayons and one real object connected to the Unit.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from Fun and Games to an adult.

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

roll. roll is one of the Unit words for games and action words.
throw. throw is one of the Unit words for games and action words.
catch. catch is one of the Unit words for games and action words.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name and act out ball actions (bounce, roll, throw, catch, hit).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can name parts of the body (hand, foot, ear, nose, shoulder, head…).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say where something is (on, under, next to) and show left and right.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can ask and answer “Can you ___?” with “Yes, I can.” / “No, I can't.”.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can follow simple game instructions and take turns fairly.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can hear and say words with the short ‘u’ sound (sun, fun, duck, bug).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself
